## Lumentum falls 11.7% on Russell index removal despite AI infrastructure ambitions

Russell removal-driven selloffs in AI-infrastructure-adjacent names can surface entry opportunities for active managers if the fundamental thesis is unchanged; the 11.7% decline warrants checking whether the move is purely passive flow or accompanied by any concurrent business deterioration signal.

- Lumentum declined 11.7% following removal from the Russell index, which triggers forced passive-fund selling.
- The headline explicitly notes AI ambitions, framing the selloff as technically driven rather than fundamental deterioration.
- Index removal creates a mechanical, non-fundamental price dislocation that can persist through passive rebalancing completion.
- The AI optical and photonic infrastructure positioning thesis appears intact per the headline's framing.
